caldera_handoff.note: Recon package prepared for teammate Caldera module. Execution is disabled unless ENABLE_CALDERA_EXECUTION=1.

auto.description: Automatically select critical/high-severity technique candidates from open services.

hybrid.description: System recommends a prioritised plan; analyst reviews before Caldera execution.

manual.description: Analyst browses all mapped techniques and selects manually.

attack_chain.logic: No open high-risk services were mapped. Continue monitoring filtered/closed results and rescan if exposure changes.

caldera_plan.selection_reason: Prioritised by severity first, then number of supporting findings.
caldera_plan.blocked_note: This module only prepares recommendations. Caldera execution still requires analyst confirmation and authorised lab scope.

ai_plan.reasoning: The selected techniques fit the scan and mode by leveraging reconnaissance capabilities and automation, ensuring a thorough analysis of exposed services and ports. The recommended techniques include T1046, which maps high-risk services and provides detailed information about their impact on the system. This approach prioritizes safe validation goals while minimizing the risk of detection.
next_steps: Rescan the system for exposed services and ports, focusing on those identified by T1046.
Prioritize remediation efforts based on severity and linked CVEs.
Implement additional security controls to prevent exploitation of mapped vulnerabilities.
Monitor the system's response to potential attacks and update the attack chain as necessary.

attack_plan.description: System recommends techniques, then analyst reviews and edits before confirming.

attack_paths.title: https_default_content_check
attack_paths.reasoning: Manual review is recommended due to the high-confidence nature of this technique.
attack_paths.next_step: Review the evidence manually and document why automated validation is not appropriate.

limitations: Recommendations are advisory and must be approved by an operator.
Only allowlisted validation modules are eligible for execution.
No exploit payloads or arbitrary commands are generated by this advisor.
